nodes.name: KeyPrefixes

nodes.name: AIProvider
nodes.description:
Registry of AI providers and their capabilities.

    This is a reference table - providers are pre-registered with their
    known capabilities. When capabilities change (new API features),
    updat

nodes.name: Event
nodes.description:
Events received from agents - telemetry, audit trails, alerts.
    High-volume table, consider partitioning by time in production.

nodes.name: AuditLog
nodes.description:
Audit trail for admin actions in Zentinelle itself.
    Different from Event - this tracks changes to Zentinelle config, not agent activity.

nodes.name: ContentRule
nodes.description:
Configurable content detection rule.

    Rules can detect:
    - Secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens)
    - PII (names, SSNs, credit cards, emails, phone numbers)
    - PHI (health information, medi

nodes.name: ContentScan
nodes.description:
Records the result of scanning content against rules.

    Created for each interaction that is scanned, whether violations
    are found or not (based on org retention settings).

nodes.name: ContentViolation
nodes.description:
Individual violation found during a content scan.

    One scan can have multiple violations (e.g., both PII and secrets found).

nodes.name: ComplianceAlert
nodes.description:
Escalated compliance alerts requiring admin attention.

    Created when violations exceed thresholds or for critical issues.

nodes.name: InteractionLog
nodes.description:
Full interaction log with configurable retention.

    Stores complete prompts and responses for audit purposes.
    Retention is configurable per org via policies.

nodes.name: UsageSummary
nodes.description:
Aggregated usage summary for reporting.

    Pre-computed for dashboard performance.

nodes.name: ComplianceAssessment
nodes.description:
Snapshot of compliance posture at a point in time.

    Tracks historical compliance scores for trend analysis.

nodes.name: ComplianceFrameworkConfig
nodes.description:
Per-tenant toggle for each compliance framework.
    Controls whether a framework is active in the compliance dashboard.

nodes.name: PolicyDocument
nodes.description:
An uploaded policy document for prompt extraction.

    Users upload PDFs or DOCX files containing company policies,
    compliance requirements, or guidelines. The system extracts
    text and uses L

nodes.name: PromptCategory
nodes.description:
Categories for organizing prompts.
    Examples: Coding, Writing, Analysis, Creative, Customer Service, Research

nodes.name: PromptTag
nodes.description:
Flexible tagging system for prompts.
    Tag types: model, provider, task, style, domain, skill_level

nodes.name: SystemPrompt
nodes.description:
A reusable system prompt template.

    Inspired by:
    - OpenAI Examples: https://platform.openai.com/docs/examples
    - Claude Prompt Library: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/prompt-library
    - Go

nodes.name: PromptFavorite
nodes.description: Track user favorites for prompts.

nodes.name: PromptRating
nodes.description: User ratings for prompts.

nodes.name: Policy
nodes.description:
A governance policy that can be scoped to org, deployment, endpoint, or user.
    Policies inherit: Organization → Deployment → Endpoint → User
    Higher priority policies override lower ones.

nodes.name: PolicyRevision
nodes.description:
Immutable snapshot of a Policy at a point in time.
    Created automatically on every Policy save (via post_save signal).

nodes.name: PolicyHistory
nodes.description:
Immutable JSON snapshot of a Policy at a point in time.
    Stores the full policy state as a single JSON blob for easy diffing.
    Created automatically on every Policy save (via post_save signal).

nodes.name: RetentionPolicy
nodes.description:
Organization-wide or deployment-specific retention policy.

    Defines how long different types of data are retained and
    what happens when retention periods expire.

nodes.name: LegalHold
nodes.description:
Legal/litigation hold that preserves data regardless of retention policies.

    When active, data matching the hold criteria cannot be deleted or modified,
    even if retention periods have expired.

nodes.name: DataArchive
nodes.description:
Record of archived data batches.

    Tracks what data has been archived, where, and when.

nodes.name: EntityType
nodes.description: Types of data that can have retention policies.

nodes.name: ExpirationAction
nodes.description: What happens when data expires.

nodes.name: ComplianceRequirement
nodes.description: Compliance framework driving retention.

nodes.name: ClientCoveIntegration
nodes.description:
Stores Client Cove connection config for a standalone Zentinelle tenant.
    When configured and status=connected, the tenant is optionally able to
    delegate auth to Client Cove via the ClientCoveT

nodes.name: APIKey
nodes.description: User-created API key for platform API access.

nodes.name: AgentEndpoint
nodes.description:
An agent endpoint that Zentinelle manages.
    Examples: JunoHub instance, chat bot, LangChain agent, MCP server

nodes.name: Risk
nodes.description:
AI-specific risk tracking for the risk register.

    Tracks potential risks associated with AI systems including
    prompt injection, PII leakage, hallucination, etc.

nodes.name: Incident
nodes.description:
Incident management for policy violations and security events.

    Tracks incidents from detection through resolution with SLA monitoring.

nodes.name: IncidentComment
nodes.description: A threaded comment on an incident, written by a user or the system.

nodes.name: NotificationConfig
nodes.description:
Tenant-level notification dispatch configuration for incident alerts.

    Each record describes a channel (email, webhook, Slack) with its
    connection details and the severity levels that should t

nodes.name: AIModel
nodes.description:
A specific AI model available for use.

    Models can be globally defined (by Zentinelle) or organization-specific.
    Organizations can approve/restrict models for their deployments.

nodes.name: OrganizationModelApproval
nodes.description:
Organization-level model approval status.

    Controls which models are approved for use within an organization.
    Ties into model_restriction policies.

nodes.name: ModelUsageLog
nodes.description:
Tracks model usage for compliance reporting.

    Aggregated usage by model per org/deployment.

nodes.name: RiskLevel
nodes.description: EU AI Act risk classification.

nodes.name: Capability
nodes.description: Model capabilities for filtering.

nodes.name: UsageMetric
nodes.description:
Raw usage metrics from agents - high volume, append-only.

    Two main categories:
    1. Infrastructure (cloud billing) - compute, storage, network
    2. API/Token usage (LLM passthrough) - tokens

nodes.name: UsageAggregate
nodes.description:
Aggregated usage data for billing.

    Rolled up from UsageMetric on a schedule (hourly/daily).
    These records are sent to Stripe for billing.

nodes.name: Subscription
nodes.description:
Customer subscription for billing.

    Links an organization to a Stripe subscription and plan.

nodes.name: License
nodes.description:
Organization license for billing and feature access.

    Licensing model:
    - Per-user: Pay per active user (seat-based)
    - Per-tool (future): Additional charges per tool type

    All deploymen

nodes.name: LicensedUser
nodes.description:
Tracks individual users for per-user licensing.

    Licensing enforcement:
    - Managed deployments: Hard limit - reject user creation if over limit
    - BYOC/Self-hosted: Soft tracking - count uni

nodes.name: MonthlyUserCount
nodes.description:
Monthly snapshot of unique active users for billing.

    Generated at the end of each billing period, used for
    per-user billing especially for BYOC customers.

nodes.name: LicensedTool
nodes.description:
Tracks licensed tools for per-tool billing (future).

    Allows charging differently for different tool types:
    - JupyterHub: $X per user
    - MLflow: $Y per user
    - AI Gateway: $Z per 1000 re

nodes.name: LicenseComplianceReport
nodes.description:
Compliance report for license usage tracking and auditing.

    Generated periodically or on-demand for enterprise customers.
    Tracks license usage, violations, and provides audit trails.

nodes.name: LicenseComplianceViolation
nodes.description:
Tracks license compliance violations.

    Violations can be:
    - Over seat limit: More users than licensed
    - Expired license: Using features after license expiration
    - Unauthorized feature:

nodes.name: AnthropicKeyManager
nodes.description:
Anthropic Admin API key management.

    Requires an admin API key with workspace management permissions.

nodes.name: FireworksKeyManager
nodes.description:
Fireworks AI API key management.

    Fireworks supports creating and deleting API keys programmatically.
    Also supports per-key rate limits and usage tracking.

nodes.name: OpenAIKeyManager
nodes.description:
OpenAI Admin API key management.

    Requires an admin API key with 'api_keys:write' scope.

nodes.name: BedrockKeyManager
nodes.description:
AWS Bedrock IAM credential management.

    Creates IAM users with Bedrock permissions and manages their access keys.
    Supports key rotation via IAM access key rotation.

nodes.name: TogetherKeyManager
nodes.description:
Together AI API key management.

    Together AI supports creating and deleting API keys programmatically.
    No native rotation - uses create-then-revoke pattern.

nodes.name: LiteLLMKeyManager
nodes.description:
LiteLLM Proxy virtual key management.

    LiteLLM provides full key lifecycle management via its Admin API,
    including per-key rate limits, budgets, and usage tracking.

nodes.name: OpenRouterKeyManager
nodes.description:
OpenRouter API key management.

    OpenRouter is a multi-provider router with good key management,
    per-key usage tracking, and credit limits.

nodes.name: HuggingFaceKeyManager
nodes.description:
Hugging Face access token management.

    Creates and manages HF access tokens for Inference API and Hub access.

nodes.name: KeyManagerError

nodes.name: KeyRevocationError
nodes.description: Failed to revoke key at provider.

    Use for fraud, abuse, or at customer request.

nodes.description:
Activate a compliance pack for a tenant.

    Creates or updates policies in bulk to bring the tenant to baseline
    compliance for the requested framework.

    Example:
        mutation {

nodes.name: ListCompliancePacks
nodes.description:
List all available compliance packs.

    Returns metadata (without full policy lists) for all packs.

nodes.name: AIProviderEnum
nodes.description: AI Provider enum for GraphQL.

nodes.name: AIKeyModeEnum
nodes.description: AI Key mode enum for GraphQL.

nodes.name: OrganizationAIKeyType
nodes.description: Organization AI Key type (masked for security).

nodes.name: DeploymentAIKeyType
nodes.description: Deployment AI Key type (masked for security).

nodes.name: SetOrganizationAIKeyResult
nodes.description: Result of setting an organization AI key.

nodes.name: SetOrganizationAIKey
nodes.description:
Set an AI provider key at the organization level.

    This key will be used by all deployments unless overridden.

nodes.name: RemoveOrganizationAIKeyResult
nodes.description: Result of removing an organization AI key.

nodes.name: RemoveOrganizationAIKey
nodes.description: Remove an AI provider key from the organization.

nodes.name: SetDeploymentAIKeyResult
nodes.description: Result of setting a deployment AI key.

nodes.name: SetDeploymentAIKey
nodes.description:
Set an AI provider key override for a specific deployment.

    Use this to override the organization default with a BYOK key.

nodes.name: RemoveDeploymentAIKeyResult
nodes.description: Result of removing a deployment AI key.

nodes.name: RemoveDeploymentAIKey
nodes.description: Remove an AI provider key override from a deployment (reverts to org default).

nodes.name: SyncDeploymentAIKeysResult
nodes.description: Result of syncing AI keys to a deployment.

nodes.name: SyncDeploymentAIKeys
nodes.description:
Sync all configured AI keys to a deployment's secrets.

    Pushes org-level keys and any deployment overrides to AWS Secrets Manager.

nodes.name: ImportDeploymentAIKeysResult
nodes.description: Result of importing AI keys from existing secrets.

nodes.name: ImportDeploymentAIKeys
nodes.description:
Import AI keys from existing AWS Secrets for adopted deployments.

    Detects AI provider API keys in existing secrets and creates
    DeploymentAIKey records for them.

nodes.name: UpdateDeploymentAIKeyResult
nodes.description: Result of updating an AI key in AWS Secrets Manager.

nodes.name: UpdateDeploymentAIKeyActionEnum
nodes.description: Action to perform on an AI key.

nodes.name: UpdateDeploymentAIKey
nodes.description:
Update an AI provider key directly in AWS Secrets Manager.

    Actions:
    - SET: Set a custom BYOK key
    - CLEAR: Remove the key (disable provider)
    - RESET: Reset to organization-level manage

nodes.name: LicenseHierarchyType
nodes.description: GraphQL type for license hierarchy information.

nodes.name: LicenseType
nodes.description: GraphQL type for License in hierarchy context.

nodes.name: CreateChildLicenseInput
nodes.description: Input for creating a child license.

nodes.name: UpdateChildEntitlementsInput
nodes.description: Input for updating child license entitlements.

nodes.name: TransferChildLicenseInput
nodes.description: Input for transferring a child license to a new parent.

nodes.name: CreateChildLicense
nodes.description:
Create a child license under a parent license.

    Requires enterprise tier.

nodes.name: UpdateChildEntitlements
nodes.description:
Update entitlements for a child license.

    Requires enterprise tier.

nodes.name: TransferChildLicense
nodes.description:
Transfer a child license to a new parent.

    Requires enterprise tier.

nodes.name: RevokeChildLicense
nodes.description:
Revoke a child license.

    Requires enterprise tier.

nodes.name: PropagateParentEntitlements
nodes.description:
Propagate entitlements from parent to all child licenses.

    Requires enterprise tier.

nodes.name: GetLicenseHierarchy
nodes.description:
Get the full license hierarchy tree for a parent license.

    Requires enterprise tier.
